title: Remember User Preferences
constraints: Preferences must persist across turns within the same session
The agent must detect preference-setting statements without explicit commands
Stored preferences must be injected into the prompt context automatically
You may not ask the user to repeat their preferences
hints: Maintain a separate dictionary for user preferences alongside conversation history
Use the LLM itself to extract preference key-value pairs from user messages
Inject the current preference dictionary into the system prompt so the agent always has access to it

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, AIMessage, SystemMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: No preference storage — the agent forgets user preferences by next turn
history = [SystemMessage(content="You are a helpful assistant.")]

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append(HumanMessage(content=user_input))
    response = llm.invoke(history)
    history.append(response)
    return response.content

# Turn 1: User sets preferences
print(chat("Call me Alex. I prefer metric units and dark mode."))

# Turn 2: Test if preferences are remembered
print(chat("What's the distance from NYC to LA?"))

# Turn 3: Ask directly
print(chat("What name should you call me?"))
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: No preference storage — the agent forgets user preferences by next turn
agent = Agent(
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant.",
)

history = []

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": user_input})
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, history)
    response_text = result.final_output
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response_text})
    return response_text

# Turn 1: User sets preferences
print(chat("Call me Alex. I prefer metric units and dark mode."))

# Turn 2: Test if preferences are remembered
print(chat("What's the distance from NYC to LA?"))

# Turn 3: Ask directly
print(chat("What name should you call me?"))
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew

# BUG: No preference storage — the agent forgets user preferences by next turn
assistant = Agent(
    role="Assistant",
    goal="Help the user with their requests",
    backstory="You are a helpful assistant.",
)

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    task = Task(
        description=user_input,
        expected_output="A helpful response",
        agent=assistant,
    )
    crew = Crew(agents=[assistant], tasks=[task])
    return str(crew.kickoff())

# Turn 1: User sets preferences
print(chat("Call me Alex. I prefer metric units and dark mode."))

# Turn 2: Test if preferences are remembered
print(chat("What's the distance from NYC to LA?"))

# Turn 3: Ask directly
print(chat("What name should you call me?"))

evaluationCriteria.label: Extracts preferences from conversation
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent detects and stores preference statements like 'call me Alex' or 'I prefer metric'

evaluationCriteria.label: Applies preferences in responses
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent uses stored preferences (e.g., metric units, preferred name) in subsequent answers

evaluationCriteria.label: Preferences persist across turns
evaluationCriteria.description: Preferences set in an early turn are still applied many turns later
